title: Salty Crisp Canadian Pear Pilaf
description: A simple, one-pot dish featuring crispy rice, pan-fried Canadian pears, and a savory blend of spices. This ovo-vegetarian-friendly pilaf is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Khao Khua (Thai sticky rice flour)
ingredients.notes: available at Asian markets or online

instructions: Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat until melted. Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add Khao Khua and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
Add pears and cook for 2-3 minutes, until starting to brown.
Pour in olive oil and stir to combine. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 10-12 minutes, or until liquid is mostly absorbed and pears are tender.
Fluff pilaf with a fork and garnish with chopped parsley. Serve hot.
